health.band: E — critical, urgent action / detailed investigation

defects.cause: Likely a horizontal crack propagating along a mortar bed-joint in the masonry, possibly driven by differential movement, thermal cycling, or moisture-related expansion of the brickwork on this exposed external wall.
defects.recommended_action: Repair soon and assess structural impact under RI supervision; given the public footpath exposure, arrange detailed investigation to rule out embedded steel corrosion or movement before patch repair.
defects.rubric_anchor: BRE cat. 3-4; crack 5-25 mm (max width 7.07 mm) over a public area; MBIS trigger
defects.report_text: A linear crack was recorded along a mortar/bed-joint of the external masonry wall, measuring 606 mm in length with a mean width of 2.28 mm and a maximum width of 7.07 mm (area 1175 mm2). The maximum width falls in the 5-25 mm band and the defect overhangs a public footpath, anchoring it at Severity 4 (Serious, BRE cat. 3-4) and constituting a plausible MBIS detailed-investigation trigger. The crack should be repaired soon, with the RI assessing structural significance and checking for any associated movement or concealed reinforcement corrosion. This is a draft assessment pending RI verification and sign-off.

defects.cause: Vertical/stepped crack propagating through the brick masonry above the opening, likely caused by differential settlement or thermal/structural movement concentrating stress over the lintel/arch zone, possibly aggravated by mortar degradation and water ingress.
defects.recommended_action: Repair soon and assess structural impact under RI supervision; given the public footpath below, arrange detailed investigation and consider interim protective measures.
defects.rubric_anchor: Severity 4 Serious — crack 5–25 mm (max width 8.94 mm) over a public area; BRE cat. 3–4, MBIS trigger.
defects.report_text: A through-thickness crack in the external brick masonry above the opening was recorded, with a measured length of 613.5 mm, mean width 2.47 mm and maximum width 8.94 mm (area 1212.8 mm^2). The maximum width falls in the 5–25 mm band and the defect overlies a public footpath, placing it at Severity 4 (Serious) per the rubric (BRE cat. 3–4, MBIS detailed-investigation trigger); the crack pattern suggests differential settlement or structural/thermal movement at the lintel zone. Repair should be carried out soon and the structural impact assessed by the RI, with detailed investigation and interim fall-protection over the footpath considered. This is a draft assessment pending RI verification.

defects.cause: Likely differential movement/thermal expansion of the masonry, possibly aggravated by mortar joint deterioration and water ingress; the orientation along a brick course leaves some doubt that this could be a weathered recessed mortar joint rather than a true crack.
defects.recommended_action: Repair soon and assess structural impact; arrange RI verification and consider detailed investigation given the location over a public footpath.
defects.rubric_anchor: BRE cat. 3–4 — crack in the 5–25 mm band (max width 8.49 mm) over a public/occupied area; MBIS trigger
defects.report_text: A linear crack was identified on the external masonry wall above a public footpath, measuring 589.5 mm in length with a mean width of 2.63 mm and a maximum width of 8.49 mm (area 1243.5 mm^2). The maximum width falls within the 5–25 mm 'Serious' band of the rubric, and its position over a pedestrian thoroughfare elevates the associated falling/stability risk. Note that the crack runs along a brick course and could in part reflect a weathered mortar joint, so on-site verification of crack depth and activity is advised. Recommended action: repair soon and refer to the RI for structural assessment and possible detailed investigation. (Draft pending RI sign-off.)

defects.cause: Likely thermal/moisture movement and long-term weathering of the terracotta/masonry ornamental cornice, possibly aggravated by water ingress along the vertical joint of the projecting decorative element.
defects.recommended_action: Plan localized repair within the inspection cycle; sound and check the surrounding ornamental units for debonding, and seal/repoint the crack to arrest water ingress.
defects.rubric_anchor: BRE cat. 2 + concrete/masonry design limit — visible crack (estimated ~1–5 mm range) on an exposed projecting element with possible water ingress.
defects.report_text: A vertical crack was observed running through the projecting ornamental terracotta/masonry cornice over a public footpath, within a measured affected region of approximately 23,974 mm^2. The CV tool did not return a width value, but the opening appears wider than hairline and is consistent with a Moderate (rubric severity 3) defect on an exposed projecting element, with a risk of water ingress and progressive loosening of decorative units. As the defect sits on a projection above public space, it is flagged for RI review and detailed investigation; recommended action is to sound adjacent units, seal the crack, and plan localized repair within the inspection cycle. This is a draft assessment pending RI verification.

defects.cause: Carbonation and/or chloride-induced corrosion of the reinforcement has caused the steel to expand, fracturing and detaching the concrete cover and exposing the rebar; loss of cover accelerates further corrosion.
defects.recommended_action: Immediate action: cordon off and control access to the footpath below, remove loose concrete, and arrange urgent RI assessment with detailed investigation and structural concrete repair.
defects.rubric_anchor: Severity 5 Critical — extensive spalling over public space with exposed/corroding reinforcement [BRE cat. 5; MBIS trigger]
defects.report_text: Extensive spalling of the concrete cover is observed on an external wall over a public footpath, affecting a measured area of approximately 44,988 mm^2, with multiple lengths of reinforcement bar exposed and visibly corroding. The pattern is consistent with reinforcement corrosion (carbonation/chloride attack) driving expansive cracking and loss of cover, and the location above a public footpath presents a falling-debris hazard. This meets the Severity 5 (Critical) anchor — extensive spalling over public space with exposed/corroding rebar [BRE cat. 5; MBIS detailed-investigation trigger]. Recommended action (draft, pending RI sign-off): take immediate action to control access below, remove loose material, and arrange urgent RI assessment and structural concrete repair.
